steps.title: Identify the grammatical rule
steps.content: The verb enable follows a fixed pattern in Standard English: enable + object + to + base verb. Here the object is cartographers, so the blank must be a to-infinitive. Eliminate any choice that doesn't supply to before the base verb.

steps.title: Apply the rule to all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘create’: “enables cartographers create maps” — bare infinitive after enable + object violates the required to-infinitive pattern. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘to create’: “enables cartographers to create maps” — supplies the required to + base verb after the object. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘creating’: “enables cartographers creating maps” — a gerund/present participle cannot follow enable + object; violates the to-infinitive rule. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘created’: “enables cartographers created maps” — a past participle here produces an ungrammatical structure; violates the to-infinitive rule. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
